# Review — OCR / Image-Text Extraction

*Generated by review agent on 2026-07-17*

## Scope

Implementation of the approved plan in
`.agents/add-an-ocr-image-text-extraction/architecture.md`. Reviewed on disk:
`src-tauri/src/pal/mod.rs`, `src-tauri/src/pal/clipboard.rs`, `src-tauri/src/image_util.rs`,
`src-tauri/src/ai.rs`, `src-tauri/src/config.rs`, `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`,
`src-tauri/src/captions/mod.rs`, `src-tauri/src/dictation.rs`, `src-tauri/Cargo.toml`,
`src/api.ts`, `src/Menu.tsx`, `src/Settings.tsx`. Verified: `cargo test` (22 passed), no
capabilities/tauri.conf.json diff, captions/dictation diffs are the 1-line `UserContent::Text`
wraps only.

## Summary

High-fidelity implementation of the corrected plan. All six blocking corrections from the
Architect Notes are honored: `current_input` is populated only by `get_selection` (not the
trigger), `ClipboardImage` is not `Serialize` and only a ≤512 px preview data-URI crosses IPC,
the data-URI prefix is built in exactly one place (with a unit test), `ocr.model_override` is
applied, both Wayland readers honor the `owned` self-read fast-path, and `process_inner`
falls back to a direct clipboard read on `Empty`. Lock discipline is correct (no lock held
across an await; consistent pal → current_input order). Wire-format regression tests exist
and pass.

Two issues stand out: a state-consistency bug where a plain window-focus refresh discards a
successful extraction, and the plan-mandated Wayland image-clipboard POC spike that was
skipped despite being the feature's highest-risk unknown.

## Issues Found

### Major

1. **Focus refresh clobbers the extracted text** — `src/Menu.tsx:195–201` with
   `src-tauri/src/lib.rs:503`.
   After `extractImageText()` succeeds, the extracted text lives only in
   `current_input`/component state; the clipboard still holds the image. The plain
   window-focus handler calls `refresh()` → `getSelection()`, which re-reads the clipboard,
   returns the image again, and overwrites both the frontend selection AND backend
   `current_input` back to `Image`. Alt-tabbing away and back after an extraction silently
   throws the result away and forces a second (possibly paid, always slow) model call.
   Fix: track "extraction active" (e.g. a ref set in `doExtractText`, cleared on
   `ghostpen://show`) and skip the selection overwrite on plain focus; or make
   `get_selection` return the existing `current_input` text when the clipboard content is
   unchanged since the last read.

2. **Wayland image-clipboard POC spike not run** — plan Step 14 sequenced it *before*
   Steps 4–12; the implementation report confirms it was skipped. Binary serve/read via
   `wl-clipboard-rs` on Hyprland is the feature's highest-risk unknown (MIME negotiation,
   large offers, the enum-cache `owned` path), and repo convention is to spike High risks
   before feature work. Run the spike (copy a screenshot with `grim`/`wl-copy`, trigger,
   extract, verify restore) before considering this mergeable.

### Minor

3. **1–9 digit shortcuts bypass the image guard** — `src/Menu.tsx:271–277`. `Enter` checks
   `isImageSelection` before activating, but the digit branch only checks `!empty`, so
   pressing `1` with an image selected activates a visually disabled action and round-trips
   to the backend for the "use Extract Text first" error. Add the same
   `isImageSelection(selection)` guard.

4. **Heavy image work in a sync command on the main thread** — `src-tauri/src/lib.rs:503`.
   `get_selection` is a non-async Tauri command (runs on the main thread) and now does
   full PNG decode + Lanczos thumbnail + re-encode + base64 for a potentially 4K screenshot —
   a few hundred ms of UI-event-loop stall exactly while the overlay is appearing.
   `trigger_menu_flow` (lib.rs:387) similarly reads full image bytes in the hotkey handler.
   Making `get_selection` `async` moves it off the main thread with no other changes (it has
   no awaits, so no guard-across-await hazard).

5. **Vision-model hint prefixes every error** — `src-tauri/src/lib.rs:579–586`. The plan
   scoped the hint to multimodal rejections (400/422); the implementation prepends it to all
   failures, producing e.g. "The active model may not support images … Request timed out —
   is the endpoint reachable?" — actively misleading for connectivity/timeout errors.
   Gate it on the error starting with `API 400`/`API 422` (or return the status from
   `run_completion`).

6. **`ClipboardImage` derives `Debug`** — `src-tauri/src/pal/mod.rs:80`. The plan's risk
   register lists "not `Serialize`/`Debug`-derived" as the mitigation against accidental
   image-byte logging; the derive means any future `{:?}` prints megabytes of bytes.
   Implement `Debug` manually (mime + byte length only).

7. **Extracted text over 140 chars cannot be copied as-is** — `src/Menu.tsx:361`. The plan's
   "just return it" path relies on the user copying the shown text, but the text preview
   truncates at 140 chars and the extraction result is never placed on the clipboard.
   Consider a small "Copy" button on the post-extraction view (backend already holds the
   full text in `current_input`).

### Nits

8. **`resize_to_max_dimension` re-encodes when already within bounds** —
   `src-tauri/src/image_util.rs:23–34`. Plan said skip; decode already validates the PNG, so
   returning the input bytes unchanged saves a full re-encode on every preview/extraction of
   small images.

9. **Double cursor highlight in image view** — `src/Menu.tsx:373` + `:391–392`. With an image
   selected and `cursor === 0`, both the Extract Text button and the disabled `menuItems[0]`
   get the `selected` class (and both claim `cursorRef`). Cosmetic.

## Positive Aspects

- All six blocking Architect corrections implemented exactly, each with the plan's rationale
  preserved in comments.
- Wayland `owned` fast-path covers both readers; generation guard untouched; `read_text`
  correctly returns `""` when the owned cache holds an image.
- Good test coverage of the contract points: `UserContent` wire format (including the
  single-prefix guard), `SelectionInfo` JSON tags vs the TS union, image_util round-trips.
- No capability widening; no image bytes logged (dimensions/MIME only); privacy note present
  in Settings; `ocr` settings upgrade cleanly via `#[serde(default)]`.
- Text callers (`process_inner`, playground, dictation, captions) wrapped with
  `UserContent::Text` — wire format byte-identical, confirmed by test.

## Approval Status

**Changes Requested** — fix the focus-refresh clobber (1) and run the Wayland POC spike +
manual checklist (2) before merge; the minors are quick follow-ups in the same pass.
